title: Weekender - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Weekender van Doesburg Creative Works launches two children’s books By ‘Are you a Snow Leopard?’ and ‘Namaste’ are illustration books for ages 2 to 5 and 0 to 3, respectively. A busy September By Post Report After a somewhat slow August, Netflix is full of new releases this month. content:
Weekender van Doesburg Creative Works launches two children’s books By ‘Are you a Snow Leopard?’ and ‘Namaste’ are illustration books for ages 2 to 5 and 0 to 3, respectively. A busy September By Post Report After a somewhat slow August, Netflix is full of new releases this month. Choose your next style muse By Post Report In another life, these characters would've made a living as fashion influencers. The struggle to keep up with your sleep pattern By Jeebesh Rayamajhi With the pandemic still around, it isn’t difficult to pinpoint why so many of us are struggling to get our forty winks each night. Conquering boredom 2.0 By Post Report Streaming concerts and virtually touring museums only scratch the surface. There are other ways to have fun while staying home. A feast for the eyes By Post Report There's something addictive about scrolling through the vlogs of food bloggers—all those mouth-watering culinary creations and recipes to try yourself, watching them cook is like a hobby in itself. Friends don't let friends watch these alone By Post Report It’s about time you take a break from dodgy weepies or cheesy romcoms to watch a movie about mates over a virtual hangout. Time for solitary walks By Jeebesh Rayamajhi Choose a route with fewer people and avoid narrow paths where chances of running into people are higher. Say goodbye to lockdown mess of a hair By Jeebesh Rayamajhi If you’re still not sure about how safe your trusted barbershop is, a set of electric hair clipper will be your best friend until you’re ready to go see them again. Have you got an e-book to bury your nose in? By Pooja Lakhey Traditional print books look great and smell good, but more and more people are opting for the convenience of the ones and zeroes of e-books. Weight watchers guide to healthy food habits By Jeebesh Rayamajhi From plant-based regime to that inspired by hunter-gatherer ancestors, different diet programmes may have different purposes but all roads eventually lead to a healthy lifestyle. Wedding in the time of pandemic By Pooja Lakhey Whether you’re Zoom-ing in from home or zooming out to attend one in person, the standard rules of COVID-19 prevention are still very important during this wedding season. Home with the pets By Pooja Lakhey Some are reveling in the constant attention that comes with their families being marooned at home whereas some don’t seem to have any idea that their owners’ routines have changed—or if they do, they don’t care. Keeping up with distant socialising By Pooja Lakhey With eased lockdown, people and places are slowly adapting to life amidst the coronavirus. Help your favourite local businesses grease the wheels By Pooja Lakhey From as small as a shoutout on social media to ordering gifts, do your bit to support local businesses in your area. Easy DIY craft ideas for creative bug in you By Pooja Lakhey If you’re done baking your 18th loaf of quarantine banana bread, try these DIY projects to keep you occupied. Build your own herb garden By Pooja Lakhey Owning an herb garden will save you a quick trip to the nearest vegetable vendor and bring the freshness of the season to your kitchen. Cuticle maintenance now in your own hands By Pooja Lakhey Think of this time as a prerequisite to getting your nails ready for when you go back to adding extensions. Virtual, the new reality By Kar Sangmo D Syangtan As you continue to shelter in one place, leverage the flexible virtual space for rich social experiences. Keep calm and journal on By Kar Sangmo Dolma Syangtan Steal some of your social media time to start journalling—one of the effective ways of self-care.
